design_philosophy.core_loop: Navigate seamless world, encounter enemy hordes, manage party tactics and equipment, defeat invading forces through direct combat
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Real-time combat against numerous enemies while managing party formation, aggression levels, and tactical positioning without class constraints
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Command a growing party of companions through a continuous high-fantasy world as a farm-turned-hero, engaging in dynamic tactical combat
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Deliberately stripped of 'slow or frustrating' RPG genre elements to prioritize action and accessibility. No traditional character classes—skills develop through use. No loading screens or level breaks—world is seamless and continuous. AI controls companions while player manages high-level tactics rather than micromanaging all party members individually.

difficulty.checkpointing: Seamless world progression without explicit level-based checkpoints; party can be customized and characters removed/re-recruited at any time
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: High agency through formation selection, aggression level adjustment, party composition changes, and tactical weapon/magic switching. No difficulty settings documented.

progression.upgrade_system: Organic skill-based progression: using weapons or magic of a type increases skill with that type over time, granting stat bonuses (strength, dexterity, intelligence) which affect health, mana, and damage. Equipment (weapons, armor, rings, amulets) provide attack/defense bonuses and stat modifications.
progression.unlock_mechanism: Equipment and items found via enemy drops, container breaking, or vendor purchase. Character skills unlock through usage rather than points allocation. Party expands through recruitment of companion characters along the journey.
replayability_hooks: Co-op multiplayer mode allowing shared progression
Character customization (gender, appearance) for main character
Party composition flexibility enables different tactical approaches on replays
Multiple story campaigns (Kingdom of Ehb, Legends of Aranna expansion, Utraean Peninsula)

world_building_techniques: Environmental exploration of pseudo-medieval kingdom of Ehb
NPC companion recruitment and integration into party narrative
High fantasy setting with lore elements (Krug invaders, Seck resurgent threat imprisoned 300 years)
Plot progression through journey structure: warn town → investigate invasion → defeat resurgent threat

danger_signals: Invasion of Ehb kingdom by hostile creature forces
Horde encounters of varying monster types
environmental_storytelling: Seamless world design reveals geography of kingdom of Ehb as player journeys
Enemy encounters and creature types populate zones
Vendor locations and NPC presence in towns
Goblin mines and other dungeon environments (robot encounters documented)

sources_used: Wikipedia - Dungeon Siege (development philosophy, gameplay mechanics, narrative framing, design intent, world structure)
